text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST AND WHOLE-BODY Tc-99m METHYLENE DIPHOSPHONATE (MDP) BONE SCINTIGRAPHY

CLINICAL INDICATION: 67-year-old male with newly diagnosed prostatic adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 (Grade Group 5), bilateral lobes involved, PSA 87.4 ng/mL. Recent pelvic MRI demonstrating bulky bilateral prostate tumor with extracapsular extension, seminal vesicle invasion, and suspicious pelvic lymphadenopathy. Studies obtained for staging purposes to evaluate for locoregional and distant metastatic spread prior to treatment planning.

TECHNIQUE:
CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis: Multidetector CT performed with intravenous iodinated contrast (Omnipaque 350, 100 mL) using a portal venous phase acquisition. Sagittal and coronal reformats obtained. Axial slice thickness 3 mm.

Bone Scintigraphy: Intravenous injection of 25.4 mCi Tc-99m MDP. Whole-body planar images acquired in anterior and posterior projections at 3 hours post-injection. Supplemental SPECT/CT of the lumbar spine and pelvis obtained for problem-solving.

COMPARISON: Pelvic mpMRI (concurrent study).

---

CT CHEST FINDINGS:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear. No pulmonary nodules, masses, or consolidation. No pleural effusions. Trachea and central airways are patent. No mediastinal lymph node enlargement. Heart size is within normal limits. No pericardial effusion.

Mediastinum and Hila: No mediastinal or hilar adenopathy. A few small subcentimeter prevascular lymph nodes are noted, none meeting size criteria for pathological enlargement and without morphological features of concern.

Chest Wall and Osseous Structures: No lytic or sclerotic bone lesions identified in the imaged thoracic vertebrae, ribs, sternum, or clavicles. Mild degenerative changes at multiple thoracic disc levels. No cortical disruption.

---

CT ABDOMEN FINDINGS:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and morphology. No focal hepatic lesions. The hepatic parenchyma enhances homogeneously. Biliary tree is not dilated.

Gallbladder: Unremarkable. No gallstones identified.

Pancreas: Normal in size, contour, and enhancement. No pancreatic ductal dilation. No peripancreatic stranding.

Spleen: Normal in size and homogeneous in attenuation. No splenic lesions.

Adrenal Glands: Bilateral adrenal glands are within normal limits in size and attenuation. No adrenal lesion or nodule identified. The right adrenal measures up to 4 mm in maximal width; left adrenal is normal.

Kidneys: Both kidneys are normal in size with preserved corticomedullary differentiation and symmetric enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No renal masses or stones. No perinephric abnormality.

Retroperitoneum and Mesentery: No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy above the level of the aortic bifurcation. The aorta is mildly atheromatous but not aneurysmal (maximum diameter approximately 2.3 cm at the infrarenal level). No mesenteric lymphadenopathy.

Small and Large Bowel: Unremarkable. No bowel obstruction or wall thickening.

---

CT PELVIS FINDINGS:

Prostate and Seminal Vesicles: There is a bulky, heterogeneously enhancing mass occupying both lobes of the prostate, grossly consistent with the dominant tumor characterized on the recent pelvic MRI. The prostate gland is enlarged (estimated volume approximately 65 mL including tumor). The findings are better characterized on the dedicated mpMRI.

Lymph Nodes: A right obturator lymph node measures approximately 1.9 cm in short axis, with a rounded configuration and loss of fatty hilum -- morphologically suspicious for metastatic involvement, corresponding to the node described on recent MRI. Two additional right external iliac chain lymph nodes are present, measuring 0.8 cm and 0.7 cm in short axis respectively, with borderline morphology. No left pelvic sidewall lymphadenopathy of equivalent size. No common iliac or para-aortic adenopathy identified.

Bladder: No bladder wall thickening or intraluminal mass. Bladder is partially distended.

Osseous Pelvis and Lumbar Spine: No sclerotic or lytic bone lesions are identified in the imaged pelvis, sacrum, or visualized lumbar vertebral bodies. Mild facet arthropathy and endplate degenerative changes throughout the lumbar spine, without compression deformity.

---

WHOLE-BODY BONE SCAN FINDINGS:

Technique and Tracer Distribution: Tracer distribution is symmetric and physiologic. Good quality images. Normal biodistribution of Tc-99m MDP with activity in the kidneys and bladder.

Axial Skeleton: Mild, diffuse, symmetric increased uptake in the shoulders, hips, and knees consistent with degenerative joint disease. No focal areas of abnormal tracer accumulation in the cervical, thoracic, or lumbar spine. No rib lesions. Sternum and clavicles are unremarkable.

Pelvis: No focal uptake abnormalities in the iliac wings, sacrum, or pubic symphysis. Mild bilateral sacroiliac joint uptake noted, non-specific in this age group and consistent with degenerative/inflammatory change.

Appendicular Skeleton: No focal uptake abnormalities in the long bones, hands, or feet bilaterally.

SPECT/CT Pelvis: Supplemental SPECT/CT through the pelvis confirms no focal lesion corresponding to the right obturator lymph node region. The increased tracer activity seen in that area on planar images corresponds to normal bladder activity. No osseous metastatic foci identified.

Skull: Physiologic tracer activity. No focal abnormality.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No scintigraphic evidence of osseous metastatic disease on whole-body bone scan with supplemental pelvic SPECT/CT. The bone scan is negative for osseous metastases at this time.

2. CT chest: no pulmonary nodules, no mediastinal or hilar adenopathy, no osseous lesions in the thorax. No evidence of intrathoracic metastatic disease.

3. CT abdomen: no hepatic lesions, no adrenal lesions, no retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy above the aortic bifurcation. Abdominal viscera are unremarkable.

4. CT pelvis: bulky bilateral prostate mass as characterized on concurrent mpMRI. Suspicious right obturator lymph node (~1.9 cm, rounded, loss of fatty hilum) and two smaller right iliac chain borderline lymph nodes (0.8 cm and 0.7 cm). No contralateral pelvic lymphadenopathy, no common iliac or para-aortic nodal disease.

5. Overall impression: no definitive evidence of distant metastatic disease (no visceral metastases, no osseous metastases on bone scan). Locoregional disease confined to the prostate and right pelvic lymph node chain, consistent with cT3bN1M0, Stage IVA disease as clinically staged. Findings support regionally advanced prostate cancer without confirmed distant spread.
